comments.author: @ChungNguyen-pq2wg
comments.text:
Hi! I’m currently working on a creative project for CODEDECO — a Vietnamese perfume brand inspired by the beauty and emotions of different destinations across Vietnam.

I recently came across your videos and honestly felt amazed by how beautifully you capture Vietnam’s nature, atmosphere, and culture. The visuals and emotions in your content perfectly match the spirit of the project we are building.

We are currently creating a campaign called “Scent of Vietnam”, which tells the story of Vietnam through fragrance, memories, and landscapes. I would love to ask for your permission to use a few short clips from your videos as part of our visual storytelling content for this project.

Of course, we would fully credit your channel and original work in the content description or wherever appropriate. If needed, we’d also be happy to discuss any usage conditions you may have.

Thank you so much for creating such beautiful content about Vietnam. I truly hope we can collaborate in some way.

Best regards,

comments.author: @khmerhope2104
comments.text:
Khmer called their own country Kampuchea. Where does Cambodia come from?
Vietnam worked with France during the colonial period in ways that led to the illegal annexation of Kampuchea Krom. During that time, France installed Sihanouk, a commoner, as a prince of Kampuchea.

When Kampuchea joined the United Nations on December 9, 1955, it was required to register the country name officially; it should have been registered as Kampuchea, but because Sihanouk was a puppet to the Vietnamese, he registered Kampuchea as “Cambodia,” partly to distinguish it from Kampuchea Krom. France later transferred Kampuchea Krom to Vietnam, a decision that Khmer people dispute, as they believe it undermined their territorial integrity and cultural identity. If anyone has the map of Kampuchea in the 19th century, you would see that the name of the Khmer country was Kampuchea, but after the French withdrawal of its colony from Kampuchea on November 9, 1953, and Sihanouk reported to the United Nations on December 9, 1955, the name of the country was Cambodia. Sihanouk acted as a proxy for the Vietnamese; they even offered him marriage to a widowed Vietnamese woman whose late husband was Mr. Ngo Trong-hieu, an ambassador of Kampuchea.

To this day, Vietnam continues to control Kampuchea Krom illegally. There are concerns that this history is omitted from teaching and discussion, possibly because it raises questions about the legitimacy of Vietnam's control and territorial claims in the region, as the government seeks to suppress narratives that could challenge its authority. The Vietnamese are very crafty; under French colonization, they pretend to work very hard, using this facade to gain favor and resources from the French authorities. However, once they gained the confidence and support of the French, they became increasingly dependent on the resources and connections that their agents had in neighboring countries, which ultimately allowed them to take control of key territories and assert their influence in the region.

comments.author: @LongHồng-m2t
comments.text: For me, perhaps the greatest thing is the love for our country that we were taught when we were children. Despite many difficulties, the people will always strive to make the country better. That is our goal. Everyone should live happily, never giving up on their dreams and moving forward. That is the spirit of a Vietnamese person. If there is a next life, I still wish to be a child of this beloved land ❤ living a simple life. Long live peace and freedom!
